user.title: Android device connection
user.description:
Enable ADB network debugging on your Android device:
Settings → Device Preferences → Developer Options → Network debugging

data_description.adb_host: IP address of your Android TV / Shield / Fire TV
data_description.adb_port: Leave at 5555 unless you changed it

data_description.shield_entity: When set, the ambilight automatically starts and stops with this media player
data_description.zone_left: Lights on the left side of the TV — driven by the left 30% of the screen (RGB)
data_description.zone_right: Lights on the right side of the TV — driven by the right 30% of the screen (RGB)
data_description.zone_ceiling: Lights above the TV — driven by the top half of the screen (color temperature)
data_description.zone_bottom: Lights below or in front of the TV — driven by the bottom half of the screen (RGB)
data_description.update_interval_ms: How often the screen is captured. Lower = faster response but more CPU load (100–2000)
data_description.transition: How long lights fade between colors. Lower = snappier, higher = smoother (0–2s)
data_description.brightness_factor: Scales the brightness of the captured color. 1.0 = match screen, 2.0 = double brightness
data_description.saturation_boost: Makes colors more vivid. 1.0 = natural, 1.4 = default, 3.0 = maximum
data_description.smoothing: Blends each frame with the previous one. 0.1 = very smooth/slow, 1.0 = instant
data_description.change_threshold: Minimum color difference before lights update. Higher = less flickering, less reactive (1–50)
data_description.suppress_siblings: For GLEDOPTO GL-C-007 and similar dual-channel RGBW controllers: automatically turns off the white channel when sending color commands

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the device. Check the IP address and whether ADB network debugging is enabled.
